item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The exploration log is miles better than the journal entries, and I'm mainly upvoting 'cause of them — the "hunt" dialogue felt very on-the-nose and the "oh no we actually fucked up" twist to the narrative was obvious from the start, given the anomaly — it felt a little repetitive when it came up in the journals too. Also, what the hell kind of person writes their diary centre-aligned?

Regardless, upvote, because most of this is very effective in terms of horror.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really enjoyed the characterization in the log, building tension without the mission being a casualty-ridden near-run thing can be tough to write but you nail it here. +1

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
The poetry of nature is never dead.

This is an entry for the 2019 INT GoI Contest for Team Inkblots. The primary authors of this article are DrCaroll and Gabriel Jade, but our other team members helped significantly as well! It was quite the journey being able to work with these fine individuals and I've managed to learn a lot about writing, both individually and cooperatively, by working with them.

As a reminder, votes on this page do not count towards the contest; if you wish to contribute to the Inkblots' total votes, you must do so on this page's counterpart.

Thank you for reading!
